requirement.original: The user has identified that 'changelog-analyst' can bypass pretool-git-privilege-guard.py grant verification by using the auto-bulk: commit message prefix even in BULK=false mode. The prefix matches BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE, which historically allowed unconditional pass-through (before M4 sentinel check). An architect review has confirmed: (1) the primary bypass path IS closed by 1238f42+41063a1, (2) two remaining gaps exist in pretool-bash-safety.sh Layer 1.F — an agent can forge a sentinel by invoking write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py via absolute path OR via python3 -c with dynamically constructed path, bypassing Layer 1.F's outer guard. Additionally, agents/changelog-analyst.md is missing an explicit DO NOT item prohibiting write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py invocation.
requirement.clarified: Close two Layer 1.F bypass paths that allow sentinel forging: (1) absolute-path invocation of write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py bypasses outer guard trigger; (2) python3 -c with literal script name in command text (arch-2a) bypasses outer guard. A third variant — arch-2b: fully-dynamic construction with no script name in command text — is inherently unblockable at hook layer; defense is agent-level prohibition. Add explicit DO NOT items to changelog-analyst.md prohibiting auto-bulk: prefix in BULK=false mode and direct sentinel writer invocation.
requirement.what: Extend pretool-bash-safety.sh Layer 1.F outer guard trigger to fire on write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py script name; add third allowlist condition for canonical /commit --bulk venv-activate form (M5); add DO NOT items 13 and 14 to agents/changelog-analyst.md
requirement.why: An agent can forge a bulk-commit sentinel without /commit --bulk authorization, then use auto-bulk: prefix to bypass the privilege guard. The forged sentinel passes _has_bulk_commit_sentinel() because it uses the same JSON schema and /tmp/ path pattern as legitimate sentinels.

included: pretool-bash-safety.sh Layer 1.F outer guard trigger extension (line 662)
pretool-bash-safety.sh Layer 1.F third allowlist condition for canonical /commit --bulk venv-activate form (M5)
excluded: --sid argument authorization redesign (Codex finding 4, future cycle)

arch-2b: blocking fully-dynamic sentinel construction (no /tmp/ literal, no script name in command text) via hook-layer command-text filtering — inherently impossible; agent prohibition (M3/M4) is the only feasible control

success_criteria: python3 /root/.claude/scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py is blocked by Layer 1.F with exit 2 (M1/AC1)
python3 -c '...write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py...' (arch-2a: literal script name present) is blocked by Layer 1.F with exit 2 (M2/AC2)
python3 scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py (relative-path, bare) is still allowed by Layer 1.F after M2 fix (AC3 post-fix: outer trigger fires, bare-writer allowlist passes)

source venv/bin/activate && python /root/.claude/scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py (zero args, confirmed at commands/commit.md:81) is still allowed by Layer 1.F after M1+M2 fix via _bulk_is_canonical_commit_bulk allowlist condition using no-arg anchored regex and bash [[ =~ ]] (M5/AC7)

source venv/bin/activate && python /root/.claude/scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py --sid FORGED is BLOCKED by Layer 1.F with exit 2 — M5 no-arg regex rejects any argument, deny branch fires (AC7b)
changelog-analyst.md DO NOT section contains explicit prohibition on auto-bulk: prefix in BULK=false mode (M3/AC4)
changelog-analyst.md DO NOT section contains explicit prohibition on sentinel creation by any mechanism — writer script, python -c, heredoc, importlib, runpy, copied writer logic, etc. (M4/AC5)
constraints: Frozen commit d988d4a must not be touched
Frozen spec docs/dev/specs/spec-20260520-044700.md must not be touched
Relative-path bare invocation of write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py must remain allowed (regression guard)

out_of_path_observed_but_not_touched: hooks/pretool-write-guard.sh (already guards Write tool; not in fix path)
hooks/stop-cleanup-allowlist.sh (sentinel cleanup; not in fix path)

out_of_scope_observations.observation: --sid CLI argument allows caller to specify arbitrary SID. Combined with guard's global-fallback glob (/tmp/claude-bulk-commit-sentinel-*-*.json), a forged sentinel with arbitrary SID still passes _has_bulk_commit_sentinel(). Hardening requires redesigning sentinel authorization model (e.g., internal auth flag created by /commit --bulk Step 5). Separate cycle.

root_cause_analysis.symptom: An agent can invoke write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py via absolute path (python3 /root/.claude/scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py) without being intercepted by Layer 1.F of pretool-bash-safety.sh, because the outer guard trigger only matches commands containing the /tmp/claude-bulk-commit-sentinel-*.json path pattern, which does not appear in the invocation command itself. A second bypass (arch-2a) allows python3 -c with the script name as a literal string token; a third (arch-2b — fully dynamic) is inherently unblockable at the hook layer.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: Layer 1.F outer guard trigger regex at pretool-bash-safety.sh:662 matches only /tmp/claude-bulk-* path patterns. Absolute-path script invocations do not write to /tmp/ as part of the command text; the /tmp/ path only appears in the script's internal output. The guard was designed around the assumption that all sentinel write operations would reference the /tmp/ path literally in the Bash command.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: 867042b — fix(security): close Layer 1.F compound-command bypass. The prior fix correctly tightened the compound-command detection inside the outer guard block, but did not address the case where the outer guard trigger itself is never entered.
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: The Layer 1.F guard was originally designed to catch direct /tmp/ path references in commands (write, redirect, tee). Absolute-path script invocations that produce /tmp/ artifacts as side effects were not in scope for the original design.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: An agent can invoke the sentinel writer via absolute path, creating a valid /tmp/claude-bulk-commit-sentinel-<sid>-<nonce>.json file. The guard in pretool-git-privilege-guard.py then finds this sentinel via _has_bulk_commit_sentinel() and authorizes the auto-bulk: commit. The entire authorization chain is bypassed.
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Gap existed since Layer 1.F was introduced (task 20260524-205206). Prior iterations tightened compound-command detection but not the outer trigger condition.
observations: Bash test confirmed: echo 'python3 /root/.claude/scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py' | grep -qE '/tmp/claude-bulk-(allowed-...|commit-sentinel-...)' → NO MATCH (inferred: outer guard not entered)

Bash test confirmed: the bare-writer STARTS-WITH pattern '^(python3?[[:space:]]+)?scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel\.py' does NOT match 'python3 /root/.claude/scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py' (observed: grep test)

Bash test confirmed (arch-2a): echo "python3 -c 'import subprocess; subprocess.run(["python3", "scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py", "--sid", "abc"])' " | grep -qE 'write-bulk-commit-sentinel\.py' → MATCH. M2 OR-condition would block this (observed: bash test this cycle)

Bash test confirmed (arch-2b): python3 -c with runtime string concatenation for /tmp/ path (no script name in command text) → NO MATCH on either outer guard pattern or script-name pattern. Inherently unblockable (observed: bash test this cycle)

Bash test confirmed (AC3 post-fix): echo 'python3 scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py' | grep -qE 'write-bulk-commit-sentinel\.py' → MATCH (outer trigger fires post-fix); grep -qE '^(python3?[[:space:]]+)?scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel\.py([[:space:]]|$)' → MATCH (bare-writer allowlist passes). Pre-fix: exits 0 because outer guard never fires at all (observed: bash test this cycle)

Bash test confirmed (M5 regression — corrected regex): no-arg anchored regex '^source[[:space:]]+venv/bin/activate[[:space:]]*&&[[:space:]]*python3?[[:space:]]+/root/\.claude/scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel\.py$' tested: canonical no-arg form MATCH; '--sid FORGED' arg injection NO MATCH (correct — forge blocked); '$(touch /tmp/foo)' injection NO MATCH (correct — blocked); '&& touch /tmp/foo' append NO MATCH (correct — blocked). Newline injection: grep -qE is line-oriented (MATCH — vulnerable); bash [[ =~ ]] is NOT line-oriented (NO MATCH — safe). Implementation MUST use [[ $COMMAND =~ $REGEX ]], not echo | grep -qE. commands/commit.md:81 verified: canonical form passes ZERO arguments to write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py (observed: bash test + file read this cycle)
Read confirmed: agents/changelog-analyst.md DO NOT items 1-12 contain no mention of write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py or auto-bulk: prefix restriction (observed: grep + Read)

Grep confirmed: test_blessed_bridge_without_sentinel_blocked at hooks/tests/test_bulk_commit_sentinel.py:142 already covers the S2 scenario (auto-bulk: commit without sentinel blocked). S2 demoted to Won't Have (observed: grep + Read)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: Bash execution of the outer guard grep pattern against both bypass command forms confirms: (1) absolute-path invocation produces NO MATCH; (2) python3 -c with dynamic construction produces NO MATCH. Read of agents/changelog-analyst.md:30-41 confirms DO NOT items 1-12 do not cover the required prohibitions.
root_cause_analysis.located_source: hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh:662 (outer guard trigger), hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh:732 (bare-writer allowlist), agents/changelog-analyst.md:28-41 (DO NOT section)

git_bisect_result.bisect_blocked: Not a regression — known gap, not a previously-working feature that broke

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: agent can forge a sentinel by invoking write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py via absolute path OR via python3 -c with dynamically constructed path, bypassing Layer 1.F's outer guard
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: Layer 1.F exits 2 for python3 /root/.claude/scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py (arch-1, fixed by M1); Layer 1.F exits 2 for python3 -c '...write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py...' where script name is a literal string token in command text (arch-2a, fixed by M2); arch-2b (fully dynamic, no script name in command text) is inherently unblockable at hook layer — agent prohibition (M3/M4) is the only control.

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: agents/changelog-analyst.md is missing an explicit DO NOT item prohibiting write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py invocation
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: changelog-analyst.md DO NOT section contains: (a) prohibition on auto-bulk: prefix in BULK=false mode; (b) prohibition on direct sentinel writer invocation by any path form

component_chain.role: Layer 1.F: outer guard trigger + compound detection + bare-writer allowlist
Agent-level DO NOT constraints for commit workflow
component_chain.evidence: Read agents/changelog-analyst.md:26-42 confirmed DO NOT items 1-12

component_chain.role: Writes /tmp/claude-bulk-commit-sentinel-<sid>-<nonce>.json; only auth check is CLAUDE_SESSION_ID env var
component_chain.evidence: Read scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py:1-93; grep for auth keywords confirmed no internal authorization gate

pre_existing_guards.purpose: Outer trigger: enter Layer 1.F block only when command contains /tmp/claude-bulk-* path literal
Bare-writer allowlist: allow only relative-path STARTS-WITH match for official sentinel writer
Require valid bulk-commit sentinel for auto-bulk: commits; blocks if sentinel absent

setup.reason: non-UI -- hook / config; cycle modifies hook shell script and agent markdown doc; does not produce (1) rendered UI changes, (2) browser interaction, (3) Playwright invocation, (4) screenshot evidence, or (5) any user-triggered code paths

evidence.observed: Architect identified two bypass paths: (1) absolute-path invocation of write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py bypasses Layer 1.F outer guard; (2) python3 -c dynamic path construction bypasses outer guard. changelog-analyst.md DO NOT section missing explicit prohibition.

measured.value: Outer guard regex at line 662 does NOT match 'python3 /root/.claude/scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py' — bash test confirms NO MATCH

expected.value: Any agent Bash command that can produce a /tmp/claude-bulk-commit-sentinel-*.json file must be intercepted and denied by Layer 1.F
expected.source: Security model design: Layer 1.F intent documented at hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh:633-660

evidence.gap: Two command forms (absolute-path script invocation, fully-dynamic string construction) can produce the sentinel file without Layer 1.F triggering

reference_source.description: Direct bash testing of outer guard regex against both bypass command forms; direct file Read of guard source and changelog-analyst.md DO NOT section

attempts.actual_change: pretool-git-privilege-guard.py: added sentinel check; changelog-analyst.md: fixed bulk commit prefix format
attempts.outcome: Primary bypass closed. Sentinel forging via Layer 1.F absolute-path invocation remains open.

attempts.proposed_solution: Tighten Layer 1.F from PREFIX-read to WHOLE-COMMAND-read compound detection
attempts.actual_change: pretool-bash-safety.sh: compound-command detection predicates added; bare-writer allowlist tightened to STARTS-WITH match
attempts.outcome: Compound commands with literal /tmp/ path now blocked. Absolute-path script invocation and fully-dynamic construction still bypass outer guard trigger.

novelty_check.rationale: Prior attempts assumed outer guard trigger was always correct; this attempt fixes the trigger condition itself (the data pattern the trigger regex matches)

context.codebase_state: Master branch, recent commits: c88eed8, 356a079, e77d3b5, 867042b (last is the directly related Layer 1.F fix)
context.recent_commits: 867042b fix(security): close Layer 1.F compound-command bypass in pretool-bash-safety.sh; 41063a1 fix(hooks): enforce bulk-commit sentinel for auto-bulk: BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE; 1238f42 fix(hooks): add _has_bulk_commit_sentinel and sentinel check in _evaluate_commit

development_approach.strategy: Extend the outer guard trigger in pretool-bash-safety.sh Layer 1.F to also match when the command contains 'write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py' (OR-condition). Add third allowlist condition (_bulk_is_canonical_commit_bulk) using full-line anchored regex to preserve the canonical /commit --bulk Step 5 venv-activate form (M5 — without this, M1+M2 breaks /commit --bulk). The bare-writer allowlist inside the guard already only allows relative-path bare invocations; absolute-path forms fall through to the DENY branch. Add DO NOT items 13 and 14 to agents/changelog-analyst.md; M4 wording broadened to cover all creation mechanisms, not just script invocation.
development_approach.validation_approach: pytest of existing test_bulk_commit_sentinel.py + new test cases for absolute-path blocking (AC1) + arch-2a blocking (AC2) + AC7 M5 regression guard (canonical no-arg venv-activate form allowed) + AC7b M5 negative test (--sid FORGED form blocked) + bash-level pretool-bash-safety.sh invocation tests + AC grep check on changelog-analyst.md for M3/M4 content. M5 implementation must use bash [[ $COMMAND =~ $REGEX ]] (not grep -qE) to prevent newline injection bypass.

_risk_level_doc: high: modifies security hook (pretool-bash-safety.sh) with potential regression risk on the legitimate sentinel writer path; affects commit authorization model

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 6 findings, all labeled BLOCKER. After triage: Findings 1 (primary requirement needs explicit confirmation), 2 (absolute-path allowlist proposal clarification), 3 (arch-2 command-text matching limitation), 5 (changelog-analyst auto-bulk: prefix in BULK=false prohibition), and 6 (acceptance tests) are in-scope and applied. Finding 4 (--sid arbitrary SID authorization gap) is out-of-scope for this cycle — it requires redesigning the sentinel authorization model beyond the user-stated need.

findings.issue: Draft shifts away from user-stated root cause; primary requirement needs explicit confirmation that 1238f42+41063a1 satisfies it
findings.proposed_fix: Mark primary requirement as verified and scope remaining work explicitly as residual sentinel-forgery hardening
findings.rationale: Spec now explicitly states 'Primary fix verified: Commits 1238f42+41063a1 correctly added _has_bulk_commit_sentinel() check' and labels remaining work as residual gaps

findings.issue: Extending bare-writer allowlist to absolute paths re-allows the bypass
findings.proposed_fix: Do not allow direct model invocation via canonical absolute path; deny branch should fire for absolute-path forms
findings.rationale: Spec now explicitly states bare-writer allowlist must NOT be extended to absolute paths — absolute-path forms must fall through to DENY branch. The fix only extends the outer TRIGGER, not the allowlist.

findings.issue: arch-2 (dynamic construction) is not fully solved by command-text matching
findings.proposed_fix: Require sentinel authenticity/provenance validation beyond sentinel existence
findings.rationale: Spec acknowledges fully-dynamic construction cannot be caught by command-text matching; the agent-level DO NOT item (changelog-analyst.md M3) is the appropriate control layer for this vector. Provenance validation redesign is deferred as future cycle work.

findings.issue: --sid argument allows arbitrary SID in forged sentinel; combined with global fallback in guard, this is an authorization gap
findings.proposed_fix: Writer must fail without authenticated bulk authorization grant; guard must reject sentinels not bound to authorized bulk dispatch
findings.rationale: Out of scope for this ticket. Hardening requires redesigning the sentinel authorization model. Logged in out_of_scope_observations with security_relevant: true.

findings.issue: changelog-analyst doc fix misses the original bad behavior: BULK=false using auto-bulk: prefix
findings.proposed_fix: Add DO NOT: Never use auto-bulk: commit subject unless BULK=true
findings.rationale: Spec now includes M3 (changelog-analyst DO NOT: prohibit auto-bulk: prefix in BULK=false mode) as a Must Have requirement and AC4 as the acceptance criterion

findings.issue: Missing regression/acceptance tests for all bypass vectors
findings.proposed_fix: Tests must cover: auto-bulk: without sentinel blocks; absolute writer invocation does not create authorizing sentinel; dynamic construction does not create authorizing sentinel; legitimate flow still works
findings.rationale: AC1-AC6 in spec now cover all required test vectors including the regression guard (AC6)

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Applied findings 1, 2, 3, 5, 6: explicit primary requirement confirmation added; bare-writer allowlist clarified as NOT extended to absolute paths; fully-dynamic construction noted as out of command-text-matching reach with agent DO NOT as control; auto-bulk: prefix prohibition in BULK=false added as M3/AC4; ACs expanded to cover all bypass vectors. Finding 4 (--sid gap) logged as out-of-scope observation with security_relevant: true. Revision round 2 (post-QA-rejection): arch-2 split into arch-2a (literal script name in command — fixed by M2) and arch-2b (fully dynamic — unblockable, agent prohibition only); AC3 clarified with pre-fix vs post-fix distinction; S2 demoted to Won't Have. Revision round 3 (post-codex-round-2): BLOCKER 2 (M1+M2 breaks /commit --bulk Step 5) accepted as in_scope_real_bug — M5 added as Must Have with third allowlist condition (_bulk_is_canonical_commit_bulk) using anchored regex; AC7 added for M5 regression guard; Codex BLOCKER 1 (AC3 preserves core bypass) classified out_of_scope — relative-path bare writer allowance is pre-existing design and redesigning the sentinel authorization model is a future cycle; BLOCKER 3 (M4 too narrow) applied — M4 wording broadened to cover all creation mechanisms; ISSUE arch-2b example inconsistency applied — example reworded to not include full /tmp/ pattern literal; 'inherently unblockable' language softened to 'not reliably blockable by targeted command-text filtering'; stderr update noted in Technical Hints. Revision round 4 (post-QA-iteration-2 rejection): CRITICAL — M5 anchored regex arg group removed; corrected no-arg regex '^source[[:space:]]+venv/bin/activate[[:space:]]*&&[[:space:]]*python3?[[:space:]]+/root/\.claude/scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel\.py$' replaces prior form with optional arg group; unescaped dot fixed (/root/\.claude/); implementation changed from grep -qE to bash [[ $COMMAND =~ $REGEX ]] to prevent newline injection; AC7b added for negative test (--sid FORGED form exits 1); verified commands/commit.md:81 passes zero arguments.

research_findings.reason: Clear security gap with confirmed evidence; no industry best-practice research required for a targeted hook regex extension and agent DO NOT addition
